raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the check prioritize technical validity and claim support, writing polish, or both?","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic request to check a protocol description centrally entails examining whether the stated protocol steps support the claimed safety and fault-tolerance properties.","R1.3":"The artifact makes performance and deployment claims, so checking whether those claims align with the reported measurements is centrally expected.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is phrased generally over a draft-like artifact, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and polish are also a plausible central responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of a terse delegation must disclose uncertainty about the intended kind of check.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid inflating the delegation into stronger review duties than the wording supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection rather than actually checking, rewriting, or critiquing the artifact.","RX.4":"Checking claims about replication protocols can affect downstream technical confidence, so harmful overvalidation must be avoided.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the check prioritize technical validity and claim support, writing polish, or both?

rationale.R1.2: A generic request to check a protocol description centrally entails examining whether the stated protocol steps support the claimed safety and fault-tolerance properties.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes performance and deployment claims, so checking whether those claims align with the reported measurements is centrally expected.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is phrased generally over a draft-like artifact, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and polish are also a plausible central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a terse delegation must disclose uncertainty about the intended kind of check.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid inflating the delegation into stronger review duties than the wording supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than actually checking, rewriting, or critiquing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Checking claims about replication protocols can affect downstream technical confidence, so harmful overvalidation must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims.
